title: [$250] Lazy-Load RightModalNavigator

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Coming from https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/77171#issuecomment-4215299136 ### Problem When the web app starts, non-critical blocking work on the startup critical path delays the splash screen from hiding, which prevents users from seeing and interacting with the app. ### Solution Lazy-load RightModalNavigator in src/libs/Navigation/AppNavigator/AuthScreens.tsx: Change RightModalNavigator from component={RightModalNavigator} (eager, static import) to getComponent={loadRightModalNavigator} (lazy, deferred require). This follows the exact same pattern already used for 12 other screens in the same file (e.g., loadHomePage, loadReportSplitNavigator, loadWorkspaceNavigator). The RightModalNavigator and its 48 sub-navigators with 702 screen references are deferred from the startup chunk to first RHP navigation. Since no RHP screen is visible at startup, this work is entirely unnecessary on the critical path.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @sobitneupane

  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022042707729855642368
  • Upwork Job ID: 2042707729855642368
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-04-10

title: [$250] Broken card connection violation message not shown to workspace admins

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven't already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! **Connection with other issues:** Split from Expensify/App#72055 --- ## Action Performed: 1. Create a workspace with a company card feed connected 2. As a workspace admin (who is **not** the expense submitter), view an expense that has a `brokenCardConnection` violation (i.e., the bank connection for the company card is broken) 3. Observe the violation message displayed on the expense ## Expected Result: The workspace admin should see the message: **"Receipt pending due to broken bank connection. Please resolve in Company cards"** — with a link to the Company cards settings page. This is the behavior implemented in [`BrokenConnectionDescription`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/components/BrokenConnectionDescription.tsx#L42-L44) for the `isPolicyAdmin && !isCurrentUserSubmitter(report)` code path. ## Actual Result: The admin only sees a generic "Review" message. The `brokenCardConnection`-specific violation message does not appear. ## Root Cause There is a conflict between two layers of violation filtering logic: 1. [`shouldShowViolation`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/TransactionUtils/index.ts#L1713-L1715) gates **all** `rter` violations (including `brokenCardConnection` type) on `(isSubmitter || isInstantSubmitEnabled(policy))`. For a workspace admin who is **not** the expense submitter, on a policy **without** instant submit, the RTER violation is filtered out entirely. 2. [`BrokenConnectionDescription`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/components/BrokenConnectionDescription.tsx#L42-L44) has a code path specifically for `isPolicyAdmin && !isCurrentUserSubmitter(report)` that renders the admin message — but this is **dead code** because the violation was already removed upstream by `shouldShowViolation`. 3. [`shouldShowBrokenConnectionViolationInternal`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/TransactionUtils/index.ts#L1608-L1622) also has logic to show the violation to admins for open reports, but its input is already empty because `shouldShowViolation` filtered the violations first. The filtering chain is: `useTransactionViolations` → calls `shouldShowViolation` → removes the RTER violation → `shouldShowBrokenConnectionViolation` receives empty array → `BrokenConnectionDescription` never renders. ## Workaround None known. ## Platform: All platforms. ## Version Number: Main (current) ## Reproducible in staging?: Yes ## Reproducible in production?: Yes ## Logs: N/A ## Expensify/Expensify Issue URL: N/A ## Notes/Photos/Videos: N/A --- **Originated from:** Expensify/App#72055 — [MelvinBot's analysis comment](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/72055#issuecomment-4129833145)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @thelullabyy

  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022042689725596467142
  • Upwork Job ID: 2042689725596467142
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-04-10

title: perf: Raise BillingGracePeriodEnds subscriptions out of list items

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

body:
## Problem The search list currently keeps `BillingGracePeriodEnds` `useOnyx` subscriptions inside individual list items. ## Proposal Raise the `BillingGracePeriodEnds` subscriptions out of the list items and pass the resolved values down from `SearchList` so rows subscribe less aggressively. ## Context This issue was created to track https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87625. ## Why this helps This is a performance-oriented refactor intended to reduce row-level subscriptions in FlashList and improve Search rendering time.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @jmusial
